name: org.machanism.machai:bindex-core
description: Bindex Core is a Java 8 compatible Maven library in the Machanism ecosystem that provides core functionality for bindex metadata management, including generation, registration, retrieval, recommendation, library selection, and project assembly workflows. The module manages org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex records as structured JSON metadata documents that describe reusable projects, libraries, integrations, and their classifications. It persists serialized Bindex JSON together with searchable metadata such as logical id, name, version, languages, layers, domains, integrations, and classification embeddings in MongoDB. The library supports semantic discovery by classifying natural-language requirements through configured GenAI providers, generating embeddings, and combining metadata filters with MongoDB vector search to retrieve relevant reusable components. Its public API centers on org.machanism.machai.bindex.core for repository abstraction, MongoDB-backed persistence, semantic selection, dependency traversal, and registration orchestration, and on org.machanism.machai.bindex.ai.tools for exposing Bindex operations as callable AI function tools. Key capabilities include saving Bindex entries with generated classification embeddings, fetching Bindex records by identifier, recommending libraries for natural-language prompts, resolving dependency identifiers recursively, normalizing languages for repository matching, and registering Bindex definitions from local files or JSON objects. This artifact is intended for applications, build automation, and AI-assisted development workflows that need a searchable registry of project and library metadata and that benefit from selecting components from descriptive requirements rather than exact coordinates.

usageContext: Applications that maintain a searchable registry of reusable project or library metadata
AI tool-calling workflows that need runtime access to Bindex records and recommendation functions
Build automation or assembly workflows that choose libraries from natural-language requirements
Systems that persist artifact metadata, classification text, and embeddings in MongoDB

constructors.description: Creates a MongoDB-backed repository instance that stores serialized Bindex JSON payloads and searchable metadata fields. Connection details are resolved from runtime configuration, using BINDEX_REPO_URL when configured and otherwise falling back to the repository's default cluster configuration with optional credentials. Use this constructor when an application needs direct repository access for saving, retrieving, deleting, or searching registered Bindex documents.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator = /* obtain configurator */ null;
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository repository = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository(configurator);
examples.description: Creates the repository from application configuration so higher-level services can persist and query Bindex records in MongoDB.

constructors.description: Creates a Picker that orchestrates Bindex registration, prompt classification, embedding generation, semantic retrieval, and dependency traversal. Use this constructor when you want a higher-level service that can turn natural-language requirements into recommended Bindex matches and can persist Bindex definitions with generated embeddings.

examples.description: Builds the main orchestration service by wiring a repository implementation together with runtime configuration.

constructors.description: Creates the AI tool adapter that exposes Bindex operations as callable function tools for GenAI workflows. Use this when integrating Bindex retrieval, recommendation, and registration operations into a tool-enabled language model execution environment.

examples.description: Instantiates the function tool provider before it is attached to a GenAI runtime or tool framework.

features.name: save
features.description: Persists a Bindex entry through the Picker after generating the classification embedding required for semantic search. This is the main high-level registration operation for converting a complete Bindex document into a searchable stored record in the repository.

examples.description: Registers a prepared Bindex definition and stores both the metadata and generated embedding so it becomes discoverable by later recommendation queries.

features.name: pick
features.signature: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> pick(java.lang.String prompt, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Recommends Bindex entries for a natural-language requirement. The method classifies the prompt with a configured GenAI provider, generates an embedding, and queries the repository with vector similarity and metadata filtering to return the most relevant registered libraries or projects.

examples.description: Shows how to turn a descriptive requirement into a ranked set of recommended Bindex matches for an AI-assisted or automation workflow.

features.name: getBindex
features.description: Retrieves a registered Bindex entry by its unique identifier through the repository contract. This supports follow-up inspection, reuse, dependency traversal, or downstream automation after a matching record has been identified.

examples.description: Loads a stored Bindex definition by its id so an application can inspect its metadata, classification, examples, and dependencies.

features.name: find
features.signature: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> find(org.machanism.machai.schema.Classification[] classifications, java.util.List<java.lang.Double> embedding, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator config)
features.description: Searches the repository for Bindex records that match supplied classification filters and an embedding vector. This lower-level repository feature enables custom semantic retrieval strategies on top of MongoDB vector search and metadata filtering.

examples.description: Illustrates direct repository-level semantic lookup when the calling application already has classification data and an embedding vector.

features.name: registerBindex
features.signature: java.util.Map<java.lang.String,java.lang.String> registerBindex(java.lang.String fileName, java.io.File projectDir, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Registers a Bindex record from a project-local JSON file for AI-assisted workflows. This feature is useful when an agent or automation process needs to publish an existing bindex.json document into the registry without manually constructing a Bindex object in code.

examples.description: Demonstrates file-based registration for an agent or automation process that has already created a bindex.json document in the project directory.

features.name: getRecommendedLibraries
features.signature: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> getRecommendedLibraries(java.lang.String prompt, java.lang.Double score, int vectorSearchLimits, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator configurator) throws java.io.IOException
features.description: Exposes library recommendation as a function tool for GenAI-assisted workflows. It allows a tool-calling model to submit a prompt describing desired functionality, technology stack, or features and receive matching Bindex summaries with relevance scores.

examples.description: Shows how an AI tool integration can obtain recommended library metadata for a descriptive requirement without accessing the repository directly.

customizations.name: Configurator-driven runtime properties
customizations.description: The library is designed to be customized through runtime configuration. Javadoc documents configurable behavior for MongoDB access through BINDEX_REPO_URL, BINDEX_USER, and BINDEX_PASSWORD, embedding generation through embedding.model, request classification through pick.model or gw.model, and recommendation thresholds through pick.score. These settings control connection details, model selection, and recommendation behavior without requiring code changes.

examples.description: Lists the main configuration entry points that can be supplied by the hosting application to alter repository connectivity, embedding generation, prompt classification, and default recommendation score behavior.

customizations.name: BindexRepository
customizations.description: The repository abstraction is a primary customization seam. Applications can provide their own org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository implementation to replace or extend MongoDB-backed persistence and search behavior while keeping Picker as the orchestration layer.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository repository = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository(configurator);
org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker picker = new org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker(repository, configurator);
examples.description: Shows the extension point used by the Picker. A custom repository implementation can be substituted anywhere a BindexRepository is accepted.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexRepository
studs.description: Repository contract for managing Bindex records. It defines operations for saving Bindex entries with embeddings, retrieving entries by unique identifier, and finding recommended entries using classification filters, embedding vectors, score thresholds, and runtime configuration.
methods: java.util.Collection<org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo> find(org.machanism.machai.schema.Classification[] classifications, java.util.List<java.lang.Double> embedding, long vectorSearchLimits, java.lang.Double score, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator config)

examples.description: Demonstrates how callers program against the repository abstraction so storage and search implementations can be swapped without changing consuming code.

examples.title: Register a Bindex document with semantic search support
examples.scenario: Use Bindex Core in an application that publishes a reusable project or library description into the searchable Bindex registry.
steps: Create a complete org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex object describing the artifact, including its classification, features, and location metadata.
Create an org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.MongoBindexRepository with runtime configuration that points to the MongoDB repository.
Create an org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker using the repository and the same configurator.
Call org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker.save(org.machanism.machai.schema.Bindex) to generate the classification embedding and persist the record.
Store or return the resulting record id for later retrieval or recommendation workflows.
examples.description: This workflow is the normal way to turn a structured Bindex definition into a searchable registry record backed by MongoDB and embedding-based semantic search.

examples.title: Recommend libraries from a natural-language requirement
examples.scenario: Use the Picker to find registered libraries or projects that match a descriptive requirement instead of exact coordinates.
steps: Configure the runtime with the GenAI model settings used for prompt classification and embedding generation.
Create the repository and Picker services.
Prepare a prompt that describes the desired functionality, technology stack, and integrations, such as a Java service that stores metadata in MongoDB.
Call org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.Picker.pick(java.lang.String,long,java.lang.Double,org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator) with a vector search limit and minimum score.
Review the returned org.machanism.machai.bindex.core.BindexInfo results and optionally fetch full Bindex records by id.
examples.description: This example shows the core recommendation scenario described by the Javadoc: natural-language prompt classification followed by vector similarity search and metadata filtering.

examples.title: Expose Bindex operations to a tool-calling AI workflow
examples.scenario: Use the ready-to-use AI function tool adapter when an LLM or agent needs runtime access to Bindex retrieval, recommendation, and registration features.

installation.description: Add the Bindex Core artifact to the host application's build so the tool adapter and core services are available at runtime.

configuration: Provide MongoDB connection settings with BINDEX_REPO_URL and optional credentials such as BINDEX_PASSWORD as supported by the repository implementation.
Configure the embedding provider through embedding.model.
Configure prompt classification through pick.model or the fallback gw.model.
Optionally define pick.score to control the default minimum recommendation score used by tool-based selection.
steps: Attach it to the hosting GenAI tool framework so the exposed tools become callable.
Invoke registerBindex or registerBindexJson to publish records, getBindex to fetch a full Bindex document, and getRecommendedLibraries to obtain ranked matches for a natural-language requirement.
Use the returned ids or summaries to drive downstream assembly, dependency resolution, or explanation workflows.
examples.description: This practical how-to scenario covers installation, configuration, and real invocation of the ready-to-use AI integration component documented in the Javadoc.
